title: puccinellia tenella subsp. alaskana
intro: Puccinellia tenella subsp. alaskana, also known as Alaskan hairgrass, is a perennial grass native to North America. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Alaskan hairgrass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support immune function. Alaskan hairgrass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
